Files

astraweave_llm/cache/
similarity.rs

1/// Phase 7: Semantic Similarity Matching for Prompt Cache
2///
3/// Provides fuzzy matching for cache keys when exact matches fail.
4/// Uses token-based Jaccard similarity for fast approximate matching.
5///
6/// Target: 70%+ cache hit rate with similarity matching enabled
7use std::collections::HashSet;
8
9/// Similarity threshold for cache hits (0.0-1.0)
10pub const DEFAULT_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD: f32 = 0.85;
11
12/// Compute Jaccard similarity between two sets of tokens
13///
14/// Jaccard similarity = |A ∩ B| / |A ∪ B|
15///
16/// This is much faster than embedding-based similarity (no GPU required)
17/// and provides good results for prompt caching use cases.
18pub fn jaccard_similarity(tokens_a: &[&str], tokens_b: &[&str]) -> f32 {
19    if tokens_a.is_empty() && tokens_b.is_empty() {
20        return 1.0;
21    }
22    if tokens_a.is_empty() || tokens_b.is_empty() {
23        return 0.0;
24    }
25
26    let set_a: HashSet<&str> = tokens_a.iter().copied().collect();
27    let set_b: HashSet<&str> = tokens_b.iter().copied().collect();
28
29    let intersection = set_a.intersection(&set_b).count();
30    let union = set_a.union(&set_b).count();
31
32    if union == 0 {
33        0.0
34    } else {
35        intersection as f32 / union as f32
36    }
37}
38
39/// Tokenize prompt text into words
40///
41/// Simple whitespace + punctuation tokenization.
42/// Good enough for cache similarity without heavyweight NLP.
43pub fn tokenize(text: &str) -> Vec<String> {
44    text.split(|c: char| c.is_whitespace() || c.is_ascii_punctuation())
45        .filter(|s| !s.is_empty())
46        .map(|s| s.to_lowercase())
47        .collect()
48}
49
50/// Extract key tokens from prompt (filter common words)
51///
52/// Focuses on domain-specific terms for better similarity matching.
53pub fn extract_key_tokens(text: &str) -> Vec<String> {
54    let stopwords: HashSet<&str> = [
55        "a", "an", "the", "is", "are", "was", "were", "be", "been", "being", "have", "has", "had",
56        "do", "does", "did", "will", "would", "should", "could", "may", "might", "can", "this",
57        "that", "these", "those", "i", "you", "he", "she", "it", "we", "they", "what", "which",
58        "who", "when", "where", "why", "how", "and", "or", "but", "if", "for", "to", "of", "in",
59        "on", "at", "by", "with", "from", "as",
60    ]
61    .iter()
62    .copied()
63    .collect();
64
65    tokenize(text)
66        .into_iter()
67        .filter(|token| !stopwords.contains(token.as_str()))
68        .filter(|token| token.len() > 2) // Skip very short tokens
69        .collect()
70}
71
72/// Compute similarity between two prompts
73///
74/// Uses key token extraction + Jaccard similarity.
75/// Returns score in range 0.0-1.0.
76pub fn prompt_similarity(prompt_a: &str, prompt_b: &str) -> f32 {
77    let tokens_a = extract_key_tokens(prompt_a);
78    let tokens_b = extract_key_tokens(prompt_b);
79
80    let refs_a: Vec<&str> = tokens_a.iter().map(|s| s.as_str()).collect();
81    let refs_b: Vec<&str> = tokens_b.iter().map(|s| s.as_str()).collect();
82
83    jaccard_similarity(&refs_a, &refs_b)
84}
85
86// ============================================================================
87// Tests
88// ============================================================================
89
90#[cfg(test)]
91mod tests {
92    use super::*;
93
94    #[test]
95    fn test_jaccard_identical() {
96        let tokens = vec!["attack", "enemy", "position"];
97        let similarity = jaccard_similarity(&tokens, &tokens);
98        assert_eq!(similarity, 1.0);
99    }
100
101    #[test]
102    fn test_jaccard_disjoint() {
103        let tokens_a = vec!["attack", "enemy"];
104        let tokens_b = vec!["heal", "ally"];
105        let similarity = jaccard_similarity(&tokens_a, &tokens_b);
106        assert_eq!(similarity, 0.0);
107    }
108
109    #[test]
110    fn test_jaccard_partial_overlap() {
111        let tokens_a = vec!["attack", "enemy", "now"];
112        let tokens_b = vec!["attack", "ally", "later"];
113        let similarity = jaccard_similarity(&tokens_a, &tokens_b);
114        // Intersection: 1 (attack)
115        // Union: 5 (attack, enemy, now, ally, later)
116        assert_eq!(similarity, 0.2);
117    }
118
119    #[test]
120    fn test_jaccard_high_overlap() {
121        let tokens_a = vec!["move", "to", "cover", "and", "attack"];
122        let tokens_b = vec!["move", "to", "cover", "quickly"];
123        let similarity = jaccard_similarity(&tokens_a, &tokens_b);
124        // Intersection: 3 (move, to, cover)
125        // Union: 6 (move, to, cover, and, attack, quickly)
126        assert_eq!(similarity, 0.5);
127    }
128
129    #[test]
130    fn test_tokenize_basic() {
131        let text = "Move to position (5, 10) and attack!";
132        let tokens = tokenize(text);
133        assert_eq!(
134            tokens,
135            vec!["move", "to", "position", "5", "10", "and", "attack"]
136        );
137    }
138
139    #[test]
140    fn test_tokenize_empty() {
141        let tokens = tokenize("");
142        assert!(tokens.is_empty());
143    }
144
145    #[test]
146    fn test_extract_key_tokens_filters_stopwords() {
147        let text = "The enemy is at position 5 and the ally is at position 10";
148        let tokens = extract_key_tokens(text);
149        // Should filter out: the, is, at, and
150        // Should keep: enemy, position, 5, ally, 10
151        assert!(tokens.contains(&"enemy".to_string()));
152        assert!(tokens.contains(&"ally".to_string()));
153        assert!(tokens.contains(&"position".to_string()));
154        assert!(!tokens.contains(&"the".to_string()));
155        assert!(!tokens.contains(&"is".to_string()));
156    }
157
158    #[test]
159    fn test_prompt_similarity_identical() {
160        let prompt = "Attack enemy at position 5, then move to cover";
161        let similarity = prompt_similarity(prompt, prompt);
162        assert_eq!(similarity, 1.0);
163    }
164
165    #[test]
166    fn test_prompt_similarity_similar_tactics() {
167        let prompt_a = "Attack enemy at position 5, then retreat to cover";
168        let prompt_b = "Attack foe at position 5, then fall back to cover";
169        let similarity = prompt_similarity(prompt_a, prompt_b);
170        // Both mention: attack, position, 5, cover
171        // Differ on: enemy/foe, retreat/fall/back
172        // Realistic expectation: >0.4 (moderate similarity)
173        assert!(similarity > 0.4, "Expected >0.4, got {}", similarity);
174    }
175
176    #[test]
177    fn test_prompt_similarity_different_actions() {
178        let prompt_a = "Attack all enemies with grenades";
179        let prompt_b = "Heal wounded allies with medkits";
180        let similarity = prompt_similarity(prompt_a, prompt_b);
181        // Very different actions, should be low similarity
182        assert!(similarity < 0.3, "Expected <0.3, got {}", similarity);
183    }
184
185    #[test]
186    fn test_prompt_similarity_threshold() {
187        let prompt_a = "Move to position 10, scan area, then attack if enemy spotted";
188        let prompt_b = "Move to position 10, scan surroundings, then engage if threat detected";
189        let similarity = prompt_similarity(prompt_a, prompt_b);
190
191        // Similar intent: move→scan→conditional attack
192        // Realistic expectation: moderate similarity (0.3-0.5)
193        // For production, would likely need threshold around 0.75-0.85 for safety
194        assert!(similarity >= 0.3, "Expected ≥0.3, got {:.2}", similarity);
195    }
196}
